Treatment Arm.evidence: The abstract on page 1 states: "patients with mCRPC associated with a BRCA alteration treated with rucaparib 600 mg twice daily in the phase II TRITON2 study." The Methods section on page 2 also states: "Patients received a starting dose of 600 mg oral rucaparib twice daily."

Type of Therapy.evidence: The Introduction on page 1 states: "The phase II TRITON2 study is evaluating the PARP inhibitor rucaparib..." The Discussion on page 5 also mentions: "Data from TRITON2 illustrate the importance of genomic screening to identify men who may benefit from treatment with a PARP inhibitor."

Type of Combination Therapy.evidence: The document describes rucaparib as a monotherapy. There is no mention of it being used in combination with other systemic therapies in the study arm. Patients were required to receive concomitant gonadotropin-releasing hormone analog or to have had prior bilateral orchiectomy, but this is not a combination therapy in the context of the study drug.

Type of Agents.evidence: The study title is "Rucaparib in Men With Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer..." and the abstract on page 1 states that patients were "treated with rucaparib 600 mg twice daily".

Prior Therapy in Any Setting.value: Next-generation AR-directed therapy and taxane-based chemotherapy
Prior Therapy in Any Setting.evidence: The Introduction on page 1 states that patients "have progressed after next-generation AR-directed therapy and a taxane-based chemotherapy." The Key Objective on page 2 also mentions patients "who had received prior taxane and androgen receptor-directed therapy." Table 1 on page 4 lists specific prior therapies.

Prior Therapy in M0CRPC Setting.evidence: The study focuses on mCRPC (metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer). While prior therapies are mentioned, they are not specifically categorized for an M0CRPC setting. The eligibility criteria on page 2 mention "disease progression after one to two lines of next-generation AR-directed therapy for prostate cancer and one prior taxane-based chemotherapy for castration-resistant disease," which implies prior therapy in a metastatic setting (mCRPC).

Prior Therapy in mCSPC Setting.evidence: Table 1 on page 4 lists "Docetaxel (castration sensitive)" as a prior therapy for 108 (93.9%) patients. Footnote 'a' for Table 1 clarifies that "docetaxel administered for castration-sensitive disease (per protocol, docetaxel in this setting was not counted toward the number of prior metastatic CRPC therapies for study eligibility)," indicating this was prior therapy in the mCSPC setting.

Prior Therapy in mCRPC Setting.value: Next-generation AR-directed therapy (Abiraterone acetate, Enzalutamide, Darolutamide), Docetaxel (castration resistant), Cabazitaxel, Sipuleucel-T, Radium-223
Prior Therapy in mCRPC Setting.evidence: The Methods section on page 2 states that eligible patients had "disease progression after one to two lines of next-generation AR-directed therapy for prostate cancer and one prior taxane-based chemotherapy for castration-resistant disease." Table 1 on page 4 under "Prior therapies, No. (%)" lists "Next-generation AR-directed therapy" (74 patients, including Abiraterone acetate, Enzalutamide, Darolutamide), "Docetaxel (castration resistant)" (8 patients), "Cabazitaxel" (12 patients), "Sipuleucel-T" (14 patients), and "Radium-223" (14 patients). Footnote 'a' for Table 1 clarifies that docetaxel for castration-sensitive disease was *not* counted towards prior mCRPC therapies, implying the other listed therapies *were* for mCRPC.
